title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
On July 1st, Stephen Megalas’s yacht, the *Helen*, arrives at the Long Island coast after experiencing engine trouble near New Providence Island. The vessel is escorted into Long Island Sound by a police boat and several small vessels carrying reporters. This arrival follows the funeral of Thomas Braid, which was marked by the unusual grief of Braid's daughter in contrast to the fortitude of his widow. Meanwhile, the nationwide manhunt for Werger Croscak and the search for Clinton have proven unsuccessful. A driver named Fox remains under strict, isolated surveillance in a small house; though he appears silent and desperate, his fingerprints have not been found in eastern criminal collections.

At the Bradwood wharf, a crowd of detectives, police, and investigators waits for Megalas. Megalas arrives via motorboat accompanied by his captain, Swift, a weather-beaten man missing three fingers on his left hand. Megalas, a tall, tanned man with an Eton College accent, greets his yacht tenderly but remains emotionally detached when discussing the murder of his partner, Tom. He is met by District Attorney Essem, Detective Vorn, Ellery Queen, and Professor Yadley.

During the procession toward the house, Ellery Queen asks Megalas if he has heard of Andrew Van, noting that Van was also murdered by having his head severed and his body nailed into a T-shape. This revelation causes Megalas to turn pale and furious, shattering his composure. Although Megalas implies he understands the significance of the "T," he remains silent as the group enters the house.
